response.justification: Candidate is a capable full-stack engineer with broad polyglot experience (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) and some C/C++ exposure at National Instruments, but falls far short of the role's hard requirements. The JD demands 8 years of C++ and 5 years building large-scale infrastructure/distributed systems with compute/storage/hardware focus; the resume shows roughly 6 months of C/C++ work in 2014-2015 and no cloud compute platform or formal technical-leadership experience. Skill set is oriented toward web/product engineering, not internal cloud compute platforms.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering background across web, mobile, embedded, and backend
Experience with high-throughput systems at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby (millions of requests/sec, low-latency DLang services)
Strong craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring via workshops, Code Katas, and Selenium course
concerns: Only ~6 months of C/C++ experience (National Instruments, 2014-2015) vs. 8-year requirement
No demonstrated cloud compute platform, distributed-systems-at-scale, or hardware architecture work
No formal technical leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers

key_factors.factor: C++ experience gap (8 yrs required, ~0.5 yr shown)
Lack of cloud compute platform / large-scale infra background
Strong engineering craftsmanship and mentoring track record
